message:
fix(schema): rollback transaction before committing already-satisfied migration

When ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN fails with "duplicate column name" (because
CREATE TABLE already included the column), the migration runner
previously kept the same transaction open and committed it. If the
failed ALTER TABLE partially modified sqlite_master before detecting the
duplicate (possible with FrankenSQLite), the COMMIT would persist a
corrupted schema — causing "malformed database schema (agents) -
duplicate column name: reaper_exempt" on next open.

Fix: ROLLBACK the transaction immediately after detecting the statement
is already satisfied, then open a fresh transaction solely for the
INSERT into the migrations tracking table. This ensures no partial
schema mutations survive.

Closes #88

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
